title: Select Game
menuMtpDesc:
Transfer mod files via USB connection
 - Faster transfer speed, but may fail when transferring folder-type mods with a large number of small files
 - For mods with many small files, it is recommended to pack them into a ZIP archive first, or use FTP for more stable transfers
menuFtpDesc:
Transfer mod files via Wi-Fi
 - Slower transfer speed; recommend using a third-party FTP client with 6 threads enabled for better efficiency
transitEmpty: Please transfer mod files to transit via MTP or FTP in the  menu first. Then tap a game card to select and assign mods from transit. The first item in the list is for manual input, allowing you to add games not installed on the console by entering a TID. See the menu tooltips for MTP and FTP details.
tidInvalid: Invalid game TID, please check and try again!
firstLaunchNotice:
Due to the Switch mod system limitations, adding mods locally can be complex. This app has greatly simplified the process, but there is still a learning curve. Please read the following carefully if this is your first time.

This app supports ZIP archive mods and folder-type mods:
· Archive type: Mod files packed as a ZIP archive. Only ZIP format is supported. The archive name is used as the mod name.
· Folder type: Mod files in directory form. The top-level folder name is used as the mod name.

Note: All file names must use English characters only. Non-English characters will cause the operation to fail.

Before use, transfer mod files to transit via MTP or FTP in the  menu. Then tap a game card to select and assign mods from transit. The first item in the list is for manual input, allowing you to add games not installed on the console by entering a TID. See the menu tooltips for MTP and FTP details.
